headline: Senate Passes Housing Bill, Bringing an Investor Ban Closer
summary: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly passed a landmark housing package, moving Congress one step closer to enacting a ban on large investors buying single-family homes.  The bill, which passed 89-10, includes an array of different policies to ease the cost of homeownership, from streamlining approval processes for new home construction to making it easier to build manufactured housing.  Senators recently added a new provision in their latest housing legislation that would require large single-family home investors to sell their newly built rental properties to individuals within seven years of completing them.
